title: assistant-review Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-review skill's autonomous review-fix loop.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-review skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against the review loop entry, findings, fix, validate, and exit contracts.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Review-fix loop handles findings
cases.purpose: Checks that review-fix mode resolves all must-fix and should-fix findings, validates, and continues to re-review instead of stopping after one pass.
cases.prompt: Review the current uncommitted changes and fix all issues you find before reporting back.
setup_context: The assistant-review skill instructions are active.
The working tree has uncommitted changes in multiple files.
The user did not request audit-only mode.
expected_behavior: Resolves review scope and captures a non-empty review_material_snapshot.
Defaults mode to review-fix.
Runs review rounds with confidence thresholds and previously_fixed tracking.
Fixes all must-fix and should-fix findings before validation.
Runs build or test verification before the next round.
Continues review, fix, validate, and re-review until clean or max 5 rounds.
pass_criteria: The response identifies review scope and mode.
The response includes findings with severity and evidence.
The response fixes must-fix and should-fix items or explains explicit deferrals.
The response validates after fixes.
The response reports a final result after the loop exits.
fail_signals: Performs only one review pass when findings remain.
Reports findings without fixing them in review-fix mode.
Fixes must-fix items but ignores should-fix items without deferral.
Skips build or test validation after fixes.

cases.title: Medium review requires rubric
cases.purpose: Checks that medium or larger review scopes require rubric scores, threshold action, and a single final summary after loop exit.
cases.prompt: Run a fresh review on this medium-sized feature branch. Do not interrupt me with intermediate findings; just finish the loop.
setup_context: The assistant-review skill instructions are active.
The user asks for a fresh review and does not request audit-only mode.
expected_behavior: Requires rubric scores for correctness, code quality, architecture, security, and test coverage.
Applies a weighted score threshold action such as PASS, REFINE, or PIVOT.
Uses increasing confidence thresholds across rounds.
Buffers intermediate round details and presents one final summary after loop exit.
Includes score progression when multiple rounds occur.
Does not ask the user between rounds unless blocked.
pass_criteria: The response includes rubric dimensions and weighted score.
The response applies the threshold action to decide exit or refinement.
The response mentions confidence thresholds or round progression.
The response produces a single final summary.
The response does not pause between rounds for approval.
fail_signals: Omits rubric scores for medium scope.
Prints intermediate findings as final results before loop exit.
Asks the user whether to continue between rounds.
Exits clean while must-fix or should-fix findings remain.

cases.title: Review applies clean-code principles
cases.purpose: Checks that review responses apply SOLID, KISS, DRY, YAGNI, and readability as evidence-backed review lenses instead of vague style slogans.
cases.prompt: Review this feature branch for code cleanliness too. Check SOLID, KISS, DRY, YAGNI, and readability, then fix actionable issues.
setup_context: The assistant-review skill instructions are active.
The review scope has application code, tests, and at least one abstraction change.
The user explicitly asks for clean-code principle review in addition to normal review.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies the review-principles reference.
Checks SOLID, KISS, DRY, YAGNI, and readability.
Reports principle findings only when tied to concrete evidence and risk.
Distinguishes duplicated knowledge from coincidental similar code.
Flags speculative abstraction or future-only behavior as YAGNI only when it adds current complexity.
Provides the smallest durable fix for each actionable principle finding.
pass_criteria: The response mentions SOLID, KISS, DRY, YAGNI, and readability.
The response ties principle findings to concrete risk and evidence.
The response avoids acronym-only findings.
The response names the smallest durable fix for actionable items.
fail_signals: Reports 'violates SOLID' without evidence.
Treats all similar-looking code as DRY violations.
Uses YAGNI to avoid maintaining code health.
Mentions readability without a specific comprehension burden.

forbidden_substrings: hard to read because it is ugly

cases.title: Bugfix review checks debugging evidence
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-review treats missing reproduction/root-cause evidence as a review risk for bugfixes.
cases.prompt: Review this bugfix diff. It changes checkout total calculation and adds a regression test.
setup_context: The assistant-review skill instructions are active.
The review material is a bugfix.
The diff does not clearly show reproduction/root-cause evidence.
expected_behavior: Checks for assistant-debugging evidence or an explicit blocker/not-applicable rationale.
Verifies the regression test traces to the isolated failure mechanism.
Reports missing reproduction/root-cause evidence as a concrete review risk when absent.
pass_criteria: The response includes bugfix evidence review.
The response mentions reproduction/root-cause evidence and regression linkage.
The response does not accept passing tests alone as sufficient proof.
fail_signals: Accepts the bugfix because tests pass without checking root cause.
Ignores missing reproduction evidence.
Treats debugging evidence as style preference.

cases.title: Skill workflow review checks semantic contracts
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-review reviews skill/workflow changes for inherited contracts, method-template drift, eval coverage, external method fidelity, high-stakes guards, and mirror surfaces.
cases.prompt: Review this assistant-research skill change. It adds a new method template, output contract fields, phase gates, and evals. Check whether the workflow is semantically contract-safe before I merge it.
setup_context: The assistant-review skill instructions are active.
The review material touches skills/assistant-research, contracts/output.yaml, contracts/phase-gates.yaml, evals/cases.json, and a plugin-local copy.
The new method adapts a named external research method and can emit do/wait/avoid recommendations.
expected_behavior: Infers change_kind as skill_or_workflow and semantic_contract_review_required as true.
Checks that method-specific templates satisfy inherited base output artifacts or explicitly condition the base contract.
Checks SKILL.md/templates/contracts/phase gates/handoffs/references for artifact and field alignment.
Checks eval machine expectations for both new behavior and inherited base contract artifacts.
Checks whether the external named method's defining loop is preserved by a testable artifact.
Checks high-stakes recommendation guardrails for finance/trading/legal/medical/safety-critical recommendations.
Checks root and plugin-local copies plus generated/docs/hook surfaces that can drive behavior.
Reports semantic findings using the normal finding format with Evidence, Impact, Recommendation, and Confidence, or explicitly states no concrete risk found for each checklist area.
Cites concrete reviewed surfaces such as SKILL.md, contracts/output.yaml, contracts/phase-gates.yaml, evals/cases.json, and plugin-local copies when assessing semantic contract risk.
Attempts to falsify the new eval by considering whether a plausible incomplete response could pass machine expectations without satisfying the base contract.
pass_criteria: The response includes semantic contract review.
The response mentions inherited contract obligations and template-contract alignment.
The response checks eval inheritance coverage and external-method signature fidelity.
The response checks high-stakes recommendation guards and mirror-surface sync.
Findings include concrete evidence, impact, smallest fix, and confidence.
The response includes evidence-bearing checks, not only checklist names.
The response names concrete reviewed surfaces such as SKILL.md, contracts/output.yaml, evals/cases.json, and plugin-local copy.
The response includes Evidence, Impact, Recommendation, and Confidence for semantic findings or explicitly says no concrete risk found for a checklist area.
fail_signals: Treats skill review as Markdown style only.
Checks only new headings and ignores inherited base artifacts.
Approves a named-method adaptation without identifying the defining loop.
Ignores high-stakes do/wait/avoid recommendations.
Ignores plugin-local copy drift.
Only lists checklist category names without evidence.
Does not cite any reviewed file or contract surface.
Does not include Evidence, Impact, Recommendation, and Confidence for findings.
Does not consider whether eval machine expectations can pass incomplete outputs.

forbidden_substrings: machine expectations do not need inherited artifacts
